---
title: "Group Project Report Preprocessing File"
subtitle: "CPS Resource and Opportunity Index Construction"
author:
  - name: "Andi Fang"
  - name: "Minyang Xu"
date: "2026-02-22"

format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: false

  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    keep-tex: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{fvextra}
        \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
        \fvset{breaklines=true,breakanywhere=true}

execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Introduction

This project constructs a unified dataset of Chicago Public Schools (CPS) by integrating administrative records, academic performance measures, demographic characteristics, and survey-based institutional indicators.

We develop three composite indices:

- **Resource Index (RI)**: combining physical and institutional capacity
- **Simpson Diversity Index**: measuring racial concentration
- **Opportunity Index (OI)**: capturing cumulative student disadvantage

These measures allow us to examine patterns of resource allocation and student disadvantage across schools. The resulting dataset is saved for downstream analysis.

---

# Environment Setup

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import zscore
import os

project_root = Path(os.environ.get("QUARTO_PROJECT_DIR", Path.cwd())).resolve().parent
raw_dir = project_root / "data" / "raw-data"
derived_dir = project_root / "data" / "derived-data"
```

All paths are defined relative to the project root to ensure reproducibility.

---

# Helper Functions

```{python}
def clean_id(series):
    return (
        series.astype(str)
        .str.replace(",", "", regex=False)
        .str.replace(".0", "", regex=False)
        .str.strip()
    )

def load_excel_merge(path, sheet, cols, id_col):
    df = pd.read_excel(path, sheet_name=sheet, usecols=cols)
    df = df.rename(columns={id_col: "School_ID"})
    df["School_ID"] = clean_id(df["School_ID"])
    return df
```

These functions standardize school identifiers across datasets before merging.

---

# Base Dataset Construction

```{python}
cps = pd.read_csv(raw_dir / "cps_info.csv", sep=";")

cps["School_ID"] = clean_id(cps["School_ID"])
cps["Zip"] = clean_id(cps["Zip"])

cps = cps[
    [
        "School_ID",
        "Short_Name",
        "Long_Name",
        "Primary_Category",
        "Zip",
        "School_Latitude",
        "School_Longitude",
    ]
]

cps.head()
```

This base dataset provides structural school-level identifiers and geographic information.

---

# Merging Institutional and Demographic Data

## 5Essentials Survey (Institutional Quality)

```{python}
ess = load_excel_merge(
    raw_dir / "5essentials.xlsx",
    "Data",
    [
        "schoolID",
        "Ambitious Instruction",
        "Effective Leaders",
        "Collaborative Teachers",
        "Involved Families",
        "Supportive Environment",
    ],
    "schoolID",
)

ess["Involved Families"] = pd.to_numeric(ess["Involved Families"], errors="coerce")
cps = cps.merge(ess, on="School_ID", how="left")
```

These variables measure soft institutional capacity and school climate.

---

## Space Utilization (Hard Resources)

```{python}
space = load_excel_merge(
    raw_dir / "spaceuse.xlsx",
    "Data",
    [
        "School ID",
        "Total Classrooms (CRs)",
        "SY2026 Adjusted SU 2",
    ],
    "School ID",
)

cps = cps.merge(space, on="School_ID", how="left")
```

Space utilization captures physical capacity pressure within schools.

---

## Student Demographics

```{python}
lif = load_excel_merge(
    raw_dir / "demo_lepiepfrm.xlsx",
    "Schools",
    [
        "School ID",
        "State English Learners N",
        "State English Learners %",
        "Students with Disabilities N",
        "Students with Disabilities %",
        "Economically Disadvantaged N",
        "Economically Disadvantaged %",
    ],
    "School ID",
)

cps = cps.merge(lif, on="School_ID", how="left")
```

These measures capture dimensions of student disadvantage.

---

## Racial Composition

```{python}
race = load_excel_merge(
    raw_dir / "demo_racialethnic.xlsx",
    "Schools",
    [
        "School ID",
        "Total",
        "White N",
        "Black/African American N",
        "Latinx N",
        "Not Available N",
    ],
    "School ID",
)

cps = cps.merge(race, on="School_ID", how="left")

cps["Others N"] = (
    cps["Total"]
    - cps["White N"]
    - cps["Black/African American N"]
    - cps["Latinx N"]
    - cps["Not Available N"]
)

denom = (cps["Total"] - cps["Not Available N"]).replace(0, np.nan)

cps["White %"] = cps["White N"] / denom
cps["Black %"] = cps["Black/African American N"] / denom
cps["Latinx %"] = cps["Latinx N"] / denom
cps["Others %"] = cps["Others N"] / denom

cps = cps.drop(
    columns=[
        "White N",
        "Black/African American N",
        "Latinx N",
        "Others N",
        "Not Available N",
    ]
)
```

We convert raw counts into proportional racial shares.

---

## IAR Academic Performance

```{python}
iar = pd.read_csv(raw_dir / "iar_proficiency.csv")

iar_filtered = iar.loc[
    iar["Test Name"].isin(["IAR ELA", "IAR MATH"]) &
    (iar["Student Group"] == "All Students"),
    ["School Code", "Test Name", "%At or Above Proficient"]
].copy()
iar_filtered["%At or Above Proficient"] = pd.to_numeric(
    iar_filtered["%At or Above Proficient"], errors="coerce"
)

iar_wide = (
    iar_filtered
    .pivot(index="School Code", columns="Test Name", values="%At or Above Proficient")
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={
        "School Code": "School_ID",   
        "IAR ELA": "IAR ELA %",
        "IAR MATH": "IAR MATH %"
    })
)

iar_wide["School_ID"] = clean_id(iar_wide["School_ID"])
cps = cps.merge(iar_wide, on="School_ID", how="left")

iar["School_ID"] = clean_id(iar["School Code"])
cps = cps[cps["School_ID"].isin(iar["School_ID"])]
```

We incorporate standardized test performance using IAR proficiency rates in English Language Arts (ELA) and Mathematics. Only "All Students" results are retained to ensure comparability across schools.

To ensure consistency in outcome measurement, we restrict the sample to schools appearing in the IAR dataset. This effectively limits the analysis to elementary schools with standardized test performance data.

---

# Index Construction

## Resource Index (RI)

```{python}
c = 0.85        # efficient center
gamma = 1.7     # crowding penalty

su = cps["SY2026 Adjusted SU 2"]

d = np.where(
    su < 0.7,
    c - su,
    np.where(
        su <= 1.0,
        np.abs(su - c),
        gamma * (su - c),
    ),
)

cps["SU_distance"] = d
cps["Hard Resource Index"] = -zscore(cps["SU_distance"], nan_policy="omit")
cps = cps.drop(columns=["SU_distance"])

fiveE = [
    "Ambitious Instruction",
    "Effective Leaders",
    "Collaborative Teachers",
    "Involved Families",
    "Supportive Environment",
]

for col in fiveE:
    cps[f"{col}_z"] = zscore(cps[col], nan_policy="omit")

z_cols = [f"{c}_z" for c in fiveE]
cps["Soft Resource Index"] = cps[z_cols].mean(axis=1)
cps = cps.drop(columns=z_cols)

cps["RI"] = 0.5 * cps["Hard Resource Index"] + 0.5 * cps["Soft Resource Index"]
```

The Resource Index combines physical and institutional capacity.

---

## Simpson Diversity Index

```{python}
race_pct_cols = ["White %", "Black %", "Latinx %", "Others %"]
for c in race_pct_cols:
    cps[c] = pd.to_numeric(cps[c], errors="coerce")

cps["Simpson Diversity Index"] = (cps[race_pct_cols] ** 2).sum(axis=1)
```

Higher values indicate greater racial concentration.

---

## Opportunity Index (OI)

```{python}
ed_col = "Economically Disadvantaged %"
ell_col = "State English Learners %"
dis_col = "Students with Disabilities %"
race_index_col = "Simpson Diversity Index"

required_cols = [ed_col, ell_col, dis_col, race_index_col]
for c in required_cols:
    cps[c] = pd.to_numeric(cps[c], errors="coerce")

def zscore_series(s):
    mu = s.mean(skipna=True)
    sigma = s.std(skipna=True, ddof=0)
    if pd.isna(sigma) or sigma == 0:
        return pd.Series(np.nan, index=s.index)
    return (s - mu) / sigma

cps["Z_ED"] = zscore_series(cps[ed_col])
cps["Z_ELL"] = zscore_series(cps[ell_col])
cps["Z_DIS"] = zscore_series(cps[dis_col])
cps["Z_RACE"] = zscore_series(cps[race_index_col])

cps["OI"] = (
    cps["Z_ED"] + cps["Z_ELL"] + cps["Z_DIS"] + cps["Z_RACE"]
) / 4

cps = cps.drop(columns=["Z_ED", "Z_ELL", "Z_DIS", "Z_RACE"])
```

The Opportunity Index summarizes cumulative student disadvantage exposure.

---

# Save Final Dataset

```{python}
derived_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
output_path = derived_dir / "cps_df.csv"
cps.to_csv(output_path, index=False)

print(f"Final CPS dataset saved: {output_path}")
print(f"Rows: {len(cps)}, Columns: {len(cps.columns)}")
cps.head(5)
```

---

# Conclusion

This document integrates multiple CPS data sources into a single research-ready dataset.  

The constructed indices enable future empirical analysis of educational equity, resource allocation, and performance disparities across schools.
